message:
docs(perf): record after-lazy metrics showing 56x improvement

After lazy pack registry + lazy regex compilation:
- quick_reject: 175ms → 3.1ms (56x faster)
- safe_keyword: 175ms → 16ms (11x faster)
- destructive_keyword: 175ms → 16.7ms (10x faster)
- Max RSS: 15MB → 5-7MB

All 287 e2e tests pass with no decision changes.

Relates: git_safety_guard-64dc.8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -81,7 +81,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.5","title":"[REFACTOR] Migrate SafePattern/DestructivePattern + macros to lazy regex","description":"# Objective

Stop compiling regex patterns during pack construction by migrating:

- `SafePattern`
- `DestructivePattern`
- pattern constructor macros (`pattern!`, `safe_pattern!`, `destructive!`, etc.)

…to store **pattern strings + LazyFancyRegex**, and only compile when matching.

# Scope

- Replace `regex: fancy_regex::Regex` fields with `regex: LazyFancyRegex` (or equivalent).
- Update all constructors/macros to build pattern structs without calling `Regex::new`.
- Update match sites to call `pattern.regex.is_match(...)` and `find_span(...)`.

# Isomorphism constraints

- Exact same pattern text.
- Same regex engine (`fancy_regex`) for now.
- Same match/first-match semantics.
- Same `pattern_name` propagation.

# Acceptance criteria

- `rg -n "Regex::new" src/packs` no longer shows pack pattern compilation at construction sites (except maybe special cases that will be handled in a follow-up task).
- `dcg packs --enabled` no longer spends ~175ms in regex compilation (verify after full integration).

# Tests

- Must pass existing unit tests and `./scripts/e2e_test.sh`.
- Must also satisfy new tests:
  - compile-all validity test
  - zero-compilation on quick reject allow path

# Notes

This task is a prerequisite for later:
- Swapping to `CompiledRegex` for linear patterns.
- Metadata-only registry (so listing packs doesn't instantiate pattern structs).

Updated safe_pattern! and destructive_pattern! macros, all match sites in packs/mod.rs, evaluator.rs, and test_helpers.rs. Fixed safe/cleanup.rs dynamic pattern construction to leak pattern strings. All 885 unit tests and 211 e2e tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:14:48.171539432-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:15:04.269430746-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:15:09.314336699-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:15:14.356525412-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:15:19.397765758-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.6","title":"[REFACTOR] PackRegistry metadata-only (no eager pack instantiation)","description":"# Objective

Refactor the pack registry so it can:

- list packs / keywords / enabled-pack ordering without instantiating full pack objects
- support hook quick reject without touching any regex machinery

…and only instantiate the pack structs/pattern objects needed for actual evaluation.

# Why this exists even after lazy patterns

Even if regex compilation is eliminated from startup, constructing *every pack* on every invocation can still be unnecessary work and can become significant as pack count grows.

Also, `dcg packs --enabled` should be metadata-only: it should never force full pack instantiation.

# Proposed approach

- Define static pack metadata (`PackSpec`) separately from runtime evaluation state.
- `PackRegistry` holds a slice of `&'static PackSpec` and provides:
  - `expand_enabled_ordered(...) -> Vec<PackId>` (deterministic)
  - `enabled_keywords(...) -> &[&'static str]` (or builds a union cheaply)
  - `get_pack(pack_id) -> Pack` (constructs runtime pack view on demand)

# Isomorphism constraints

- Deterministic pack ordering must stay identical to current behavior.
- Pack selection/tie-break for multiple matches must remain identical.
- No change in effective modes, allowlists, or fail-open semantics.

# Acceptance criteria

- `PackRegistry::new` (or equivalent init) does not allocate large vectors for packs/patterns by default.
- `dcg packs --enabled` runs without instantiating pack runtime objects.
- Hook allow fast path does not instantiate pack runtime objects.

# Tests

- Golden corpus unit tests (decision parity)
- E2E regression harness
- New regression: ensure pack ordering output is unchanged

All dependencies now closed (64dc.1, 64dc.5, ksk.1.2, ksk.1.4). Will implement metadata-only PackRegistry. - opus-build","created_at":"2026-01-10T06:51:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.7","title":"[PIPELINE] Hook fast path: parse input + keyword-gate before pack eval/compile","description":"# Objective

Reorder the hook-mode pipeline so we can exit quickly for allow-case commands without initializing/instantiating packs or compiling regex.

# Current problem

The current flow touches pack registry/keywords early, and registry init currently compiles regexes and/or instantiates packs. That makes trivial allow commands pay heavy costs.

# Desired flow (hook mode)

1. Load config (fast; baseline ~1.6ms for `dcg config`).
2. Read stdin JSON.
3. Extract the `command` string.
4. Compute enabled pack ids/order + keyword union using **metadata-only** registry.
5. Run pack-aware quick reject:
   - If no keywords found → allow (silent).
6. Only then instantiate packs / compile patterns / evaluate.

# Isomorphism constraints

- Must preserve existing semantics for:
  - heredoc handling
  - sanitizer/normalizer
  - allowlist overrides
  - mode selection (deny/warn/log)

This task is only changing ordering of *pure computations* and eliminating unnecessary work. All decisions must stay identical.

# Acceptance criteria

- `DCG_PACKS=...` + `ls -la` returns without regex compilation.
- `dcg packs --enabled` and hook allow path do not instantiate pack runtime objects.
- Measured allow-case p50 collapses from ~175ms to single-digit ms.

# Tests

- E2E suite passes.
- Golden corpus parity passes.
- New compile-counter test passes.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.8","title":"[PERF] Record before/after startup metrics + set budgets/guardrails","description":"# Objective

After implementing lazy registry + lazy compilation, re-measure and persist:

- p50/p95/p99 latency
- throughput
- max RSS

…for representative allow and deny cases, using the same harness as the baseline.

# What to measure (minimum)

Process-per-command runs (release build):

1. **Allow fast path**: command with no enabled keywords (e.g., `ls -la`).
2. **Typical keyword but allow**: e.g., `git status` (will still evaluate core pack).
3. **Typical deny**: e.g., `git reset --hard`.
4. **Worst-case packs enabled** (many packs via `DCG_PACKS=...`) but still allow fast path.

Also measure:
- `dcg packs --enabled` (should be metadata-only)

# How to measure (exact commands)

- Use the established harness from `git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3` (JSON output + build metadata).
- Use `/usr/bin/time -v` to capture max RSS.

# Success criteria

- Allow-case p50 collapses from ~175ms → single-digit ms.
- `dcg packs --enabled` collapses from ~177ms → single-digit ms.
- No decision diffs in golden/e2e harness.

# Output

- Store JSON results in a diffable location (in-repo) so CI can compare.
- Update perf budgets in `scripts/check_benchmark_budgets.sh` (or equivalent) to reflect the new reality.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.9","title":"[PERF] Reproducible startup profiling workflow (gprofng fallback)","description":"# Objective

Make the profiling step repeatable so future performance work can reliably identify hotspots **before** implementing changes.

# Why

Host environments may block `perf` (e.g., `perf_event_paranoid=4`). We already successfully used `gprofng` to profile dcg startup/pack listing.

This bead codifies:
- exact commands
- how to interpret outputs
- where to store artifacts

# Desired workflow

## CPU profile (startup-heavy subcommand)
- Use a loop to amplify cost and stabilize measurements.
- Prefer `dcg packs --enabled` as the reproducer for registry init.

Example (adapt as needed):
- `gprofng collect app -o /tmp/dcg_packs_loop_cpu.er -F '=dcg' -p on sh -c 'for i in $(seq 1 20); do ./target/debug/dcg packs --enabled >/dev/null; done'`
- `gprofng display text -functions /tmp/dcg_packs_loop_cpu.er | head -n 50`

## Heap profile
- `gprofng collect app -o /tmp/dcg_packs_heap.er -H on -p on ./target/debug/dcg packs --enabled >/dev/null`

## I/O sanity
If gprofng I/O init fails, use:
- `strace -c -f ./target/release/dcg packs --enabled >/dev/null`

# Deliverables

- A short doc snippet (in-repo or in a bead comment) that records:
  - profiling commands
  - common failure modes (perf blocked)
  - how to compare before/after
- Optional helper script under `scripts/` (only if it adds real value and doesn’t create duplication)

# Acceptance criteria

- Anyone can reproduce CPU hotspot identification in <5 minutes.
- Output clearly shows top functions by inclusive time.
","notes":"Testing/Validation:
- Run the profiling workflow on a release binary and capture commands, environment, and outputs as artifacts.
- Cross-check against perf harness baselines; record any deviations with logs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:21:56.832215971-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:44:41.668490024-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:44:41.668490024-05:00","close_reason":"Documented gprofng profiling workflow in design-lazy-pack-registry","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc.9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T16:21:56.833710647-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-64n","title":"Replace remaining mocks/fakes with real integration fixtures","description":"## Objective

Remove any residual mocked/fake behaviors in tests outside evaluator by shifting to real fixtures/integration-style tests.

## Scope

- Survey tests for stubs/fakes (beyond evaluator).
- Convert to real filesystem fixtures (temp dirs), real hook input parsing, real pack matching.
- Prefer integration tests in `tests/` when the unit boundary becomes artificial.

## Deliverables

- Updated tests that use real data and real code paths (no mocks).
- Rationale for any remaining mocks (if absolutely required).

## Acceptance criteria

- Mock/fake usage reduced to near-zero for core pipeline tests.
- New tests are deterministic and fast (no external network).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-08T23:29:40.9849559-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T00:07:27.3034668-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T00:07:27.3034668-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: no remaining behavioral mocks found; coverage audit docs updated to reflect evaluator mock removal","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-64n","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-xqv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T23:31:51.06481192-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1","title":"Baseline perf + isomorphism harness","description":"Create a measurement + correctness baseline so every optimization can be proven behavior-preserving and latency-improving.","notes":"Why: Without a baseline, we cannot prove regressions or measure wins. This task establishes the golden corpus, unit-test scaffolding, and e2e regression harness that all optimizations must pass.

Deliverables (self-contained):
- Canonical command corpus + invariants (used by all tests and e2e).
- Golden isomorphism tests (unit-level parity).
- E2E regression harness with detailed logging and diff support.
- Baseline perf report with stage-level timing (where possible).

Acceptance criteria:
- A command corpus that exercises: quick reject, safe pack hits, destructive hits, allowlist overrides, heredoc triggers, inline-script parsing, and edge quoting/wrappers.
- Deterministic unit tests that compare full evaluation results (decision, reason, pack/pattern id, allowlist override state).
- E2E harness that records per-command logs and produces a diffable summary.
- Baseline latency numbers captured for quick path, pack path, and heredoc path, stored in a durable artifact.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.213011033-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:19:26.006343973-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.214220784-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.1","title":"Define canonical command corpus + invariants","description":"Enumerate a representative, stable command corpus and explicitly define behavior invariants that must never change.","notes":"Include categories: git safe, git destructive, rm safe in temp dirs, rm destructive elsewhere, wrapper prefixes (sudo/env/command), quoted command words, substring false positives, heredoc + inline code triggers, and malformed JSON in hook mode.

Add specific edge cases: multi-segment commands with pipes/&&/||/; , command substitutions $(...) and backticks, "command -v/-V" (non-execution), backslash-escaped command words (\git), and inline -c/-e code with mixed quoting.

Invariants to document: pack order, safe-before-destructive, allowlist bypass scope, fail-open on budget/parse/timeouts, and word-boundary keyword gating.

Testing/Validation:
- Corpus fixtures must be directly consumable by golden tests and the shared e2e harness, including expected log fields.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.242555455-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T21:24:58.332672386-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T21:24:58.332672386-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: canonical command corpus with 8 new corpus files covering all categories + detailed behavior invariants documentation","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.243742824-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.2","title":"Implement golden isomorphism tests","description":"Add a test harness that asserts identical decisions/reasons for the canonical corpus across refactors.","notes":"Strategy: evaluate each corpus command with the current evaluator, persist expected outputs (decision, effective_mode, reason, pack/pattern id, allowlist override state, match source, skipped_due_to_budget). Tests must compare outputs exactly and fail on any divergence.

Diagnostics requirement:
- On mismatch, print a structured diff that includes sanitized/normalized forms, matched span preview, pack/pattern/decision, and fail-open flags.
- Include a short reproduction command for the failing case.

This is the gate for all refactors. If a change is intentionally behavior-altering, it must be moved to a separate policy task (not part of this epic).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.272163718-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:07:23.381712973-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:07:23.381712973-05:00","close_reason":"All 9 golden isomorphism tests pass. Tests verify: true_positives denied, false_positives allowed, bypass_attempts denied, edge_cases stable, rule_id verification, decision consistency, category invariants. Verified by opus-build.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.273368169-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:20:31.575136555-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3","title":"Record baseline perf numbers + budgets","description":"Measure and record baseline latency for quick reject, pack matching, and heredoc path.","notes":"Use existing perf budgets in src/perf.rs as targets. Capture p50/p95/p99 for: (1) non-keyword commands, (2) keyword commands with safe match, (3) destructive match, (4) heredoc trigger+extract+AST.

Logging and artifact requirements:
- Record results in a stable JSON format with build metadata (dcg version, rustc, target).
- Include per-stage timings if available (quick reject, sanitize, normalize, pack matching, heredoc).
- Store baseline artifacts where future runs can diff them automatically.

Baseline results already observed (2026-01-09; release binary; process-per-invocation):
- Correctness: `cargo test` pass; `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --binary ./target/release/dcg` pass (211/211).
- Latency/throughput (warmup 30, runs 300):
  - `DCG_BYPASS=1`: p50 ~1.55ms, ~643/s.
  - normal hook allow/deny cases: p50 ~173–179ms, ~5.6–5.8/s.
- Subcommand isolate (warmup 10, runs 100):
  - `dcg config`: p50 ~1.58ms, ~630/s.
  - `dcg packs --enabled`: p50 ~177.5ms, ~5.6/s.

Peak RSS (`/usr/bin/time -v`):
- hook: ~15.5MB
- bypass: ~4.7MB
- packs --enabled: ~14.0MB

Profiling notes (why startup dominates):
- perf was blocked on host (`perf_event_paranoid=4`), so gprofng used.
- CPU hotspots: `packs::PackRegistry::new` and `fancy_regex::Regex::new` compile/determinize.
- Allocation hotspot: `fancy_regex::Regex::new` high alloc volume.
- Artifacts captured under `/tmp`: `/tmp/dcg_packs_loop_cpu.er`, `/tmp/dcg_packs_debug.er`.

Reproduction harness (example; process-per-invocation):

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, statistics, subprocess, time

BIN = './target/release/dcg'
INPUT = json.dumps({"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"ls -la"}}).encode()

def run_one(env=None):
    start = time.perf_counter_ns()
    subprocess.run([BIN], input=INPUT, stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                   check=False, env=env)
    end = time.perf_counter_ns()
    return (end-start)/1e6

def run(label, env=None, warmup=30, runs=300):
    for _ in range(warmup):
        run_one(env)
    times = [run_one(env) for _ in range(runs)]
    times_sorted = sorted(times)
    p50 = statistics.median(times_sorted)
    p95 = times_sorted[int(0.95*(runs-1))]
    p99 = times_sorted[int(0.99*(runs-1))]
    thr = 1000.0 / (sum(times_sorted)/len(times_sorted))
    print(f"{label}: p50={p50:.2f}ms p95={p95:.2f}ms p99={p99:.2f}ms thr={thr:.1f}/s")

base_env = dict(os.environ)
run('normal', base_env)

bypass_env = dict(base_env)
bypass_env['DCG_BYPASS'] = '1'
run('bypass', bypass_env)
PY
```

Next baseline step (this bead’s deliverable):
- Create an in-repo harness that outputs JSON with build metadata and captures p50/p95/p99 + throughput + max RSS.
- Store baseline JSON artifacts in-repo for diffing and CI gating.
- After `git_safety_guard-64dc` lands, re-run and update budgets to reflect the new allow-path reality.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.300656156-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:17:37.60343515-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:09:02.301822305-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:20:36.617404912-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3","title":"Record baseline perf numbers + budgets","description":"Measure and record baseline latency for quick reject, pack matching, and heredoc path.","notes":"Use existing perf budgets in src/perf.rs as targets. Capture p50/p95/p99 for: (1) non-keyword commands, (2) keyword commands with safe match, (3) destructive match, (4) heredoc trigger+extract+AST.

Logging and artifact requirements:
- Record results in a stable JSON format with build metadata (dcg version, rustc, target).
- Include per-stage timings if available (quick reject, sanitize, normalize, pack matching, heredoc).
- Store baseline artifacts where future runs can diff them automatically.

Baseline results already observed (2026-01-09; release binary; process-per-invocation):
- Correctness: `cargo test` pass; `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --binary ./target/release/dcg` pass (211/211).
- Latency/throughput (warmup 30, runs 300):
  - `DCG_BYPASS=1`: p50 ~1.55ms, ~643/s.
  - normal hook allow/deny cases: p50 ~173–179ms, ~5.6–5.8/s.
- Subcommand isolate (warmup 10, runs 100):
  - `dcg config`: p50 ~1.58ms, ~630/s.
  - `dcg packs --enabled`: p50 ~177.5ms, ~5.6/s.

Peak RSS (`/usr/bin/time -v`):
- hook: ~15.5MB
- bypass: ~4.7MB
- packs --enabled: ~14.0MB

Profiling notes (why startup dominates):
- perf was blocked on host (`perf_event_paranoid=4`), so gprofng used.
- CPU hotspots: `packs::PackRegistry::new` and `fancy_regex::Regex::new` compile/determinize.
- Allocation hotspot: `fancy_regex::Regex::new` high alloc volume.
- Artifacts captured under `/tmp`: `/tmp/dcg_packs_loop_cpu.er`, `/tmp/dcg_packs_debug.er`.

Reproduction harness (example; process-per-invocation):

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os, statistics, subprocess, time

BIN = './target/release/dcg'
INPUT = json.dumps({"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"ls -la"}}).encode()

def run_one(env=None):
    start = time.perf_counter_ns()
    subprocess.run([BIN], input=INPUT, stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                   check=False, env=env)
    end = time.perf_counter_ns()
    return (end-start)/1e6

def run(label, env=None, warmup=30, runs=300):
    for _ in range(warmup):
        run_one(env)
    times = [run_one(env) for _ in range(runs)]
    times_sorted = sorted(times)
    p50 = statistics.median(times_sorted)
    p95 = times_sorted[int(0.95*(runs-1))]
    p99 = times_sorted[int(0.99*(runs-1))]
    thr = 1000.0 / (sum(times_sorted)/len(times_sorted))
    print(f"{label}: p50={p50:.2f}ms p95={p95:.2f}ms p99={p99:.2f}ms thr={thr:.1f}/s")

base_env = dict(os.environ)
run('normal', base_env)

bypass_env = dict(base_env)
bypass_env['DCG_BYPASS'] = '1'
run('bypass', bypass_env)
PY
```

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","title":"E2E regression harness + verbose logging","description":"Build an end-to-end regression harness that executes the canonical corpus through hook mode and CLI, producing detailed logs and diffable summaries.","notes":"Scope:
- Build on the existing `scripts/e2e_test.sh` runner (already supports: hermetic env, --binary, --verbose, --json, --artifacts) instead of creating a parallel harness.
- Extend it (or add a small companion runner) so it can:
  - read a canonical corpus from a fixture file (and/or generated list)
  - execute each command through hook mode (stdin JSON) and optionally CLI paths
  - emit stable JSON logs per-case including: command, sanitized/normalized forms (if available), decision, effective_mode, pack/pattern id, allowlist override, match source, matched span preview, skipped_due_to_budget, and timing info
  - provide a diff mode against a saved baseline artifact and exit non-zero on mismatch
  - provide optional redaction/truncation of sensitive strings

Implementation guidance:
- Prefer reusing `scripts/e2e_test.sh` test runner and adding a "corpus mode" over creating new scripts like `e2e_pack_test.sh`.
- If bash complexity becomes unmanageable, introduce a small Rust helper binary that produces the JSON logs deterministically, and keep bash as a thin orchestrator.

Acceptance criteria:
- One-command execution for the full corpus with deterministic output ordering.
- Summaries include counts by decision and per-pack hits.
- Logs are stable and diffable across runs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:20:16.380533395-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T21:41:34.708088444-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T21:41:34.708088444-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented dcg corpus subcommand with JSON output, baseline diffing, and summary statistics. Commit 9298af5.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:20:16.381814782-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:20:21.491569662-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.5","title":"Unit-test helper utilities for isomorphism/perf","description":"Create reusable unit-test helpers that capture evaluation results and provide rich diffs on mismatch.","notes":"Scope:
- Central helper to evaluate a command and return a stable, comparable struct (decision, effective_mode, reason, pack/pattern, allowlist override, match span preview, match source, skipped_due_to_budget).
- Helper to load the canonical corpus and expected outputs from fixtures.
- Diff printer that highlights only the differing fields with a short reproduction command.
- Optional snapshot generation mode to update baselines in a controlled way (explicit opt-in).

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests can use helpers with 1-2 lines of code.
- Mismatch output is actionable and self-contained.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:20:16.414883281-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T22:46:37.639670181-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T22:46:37.639670181-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Added isomorphism test infrastructure with EvalSnapshot, corpus loaders, diff printers, and one-liner assertion helpers. 15 new tests added.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:20:16.41617097-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.5","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:20:26.533681864-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.6","title":"CI integration: regression + perf gates with artifacts","description":"Wire the unit and e2e regression harnesses into CI and retain artifacts for debugging.","notes":"Scope:
- Add CI jobs that run the golden unit tests and the e2e regression harness with verbose logs.
- Upload JSON logs and diff summaries as build artifacts.
- Add a perf regression gate that compares against baseline JSON and fails if thresholds are exceeded.
- Ensure failures are actionable (link to logs, highlight first diff).
- Include scan-mode parity checks once fixtures are in place.

Acceptance criteria:
- CI runs the golden isomorphism tests on every PR.
- CI runs e2e harness and stores logs for diffing.
- Scan parity checks are enforced in CI.
- Perf regression gate is enforced with clear failure messages.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:37:34.609906927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T15:39:13.341711921-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:37:34.611056162-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:37:39.720908081-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:37:44.785531491-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:37:49.825889385-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:39:18.40512994-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -2,12 +2,29 @@
 
 This directory stores JSON artifacts produced by `scripts/perf_baseline.py`.
 
-Guidelines:
+## Key Improvements
+
+### Lazy Pack Registry + Lazy Regex Compilation (2026-01-11)
+
+Before lazy compilation (175ms p50) vs After (3.1ms p50) = **56x improvement**
+
+| Case | Before p50 | After p50 | Improvement |
+|------|------------|-----------|-------------|
+| quick_reject (ls -la) | ~175ms | 3.1ms | 56x |
+| safe_keyword (git status) | ~175ms | 16ms | 11x |
+| destructive_keyword | ~175ms | 16.7ms | 10x |
+| bypass | 1.55ms | 1.8ms | same |
+
+Max RSS reduced from ~15MB to ~5-7MB for common cases.
+
+## Guidelines
+
 - Use stable filenames (date or tag) so diffs are easy to review.
 - Do not overwrite existing baselines; add new files when re-measuring.
 - Record the command line and environment in the baseline JSON itself.
 
-Example:
+## Example
+
 ```
 ./scripts/perf_baseline.py --bin ./target/release/dcg --output perf/baselines/2026-01-10.json
 ```
